sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.34 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: By definition, a locally compact group has the Kazhdan$T$-property\/ if the trivial representation is an isolated point in the natural topological space of unitary representations of the group. Does there exist a profinite group with two dense discrete subgroups one of which is amenable, and the other has the Kazhdan$T$-property? See (A.\,Lubotzky, Discrete groups, expanding graphs and invariant m...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
